mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates month-over-month, signaling strengthening labor demand and consumer income expansion. This drives equity valuations upward through improved earnings expectations and reduced recession risk over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2008 recovery phase with PAYEMS at 130539k suggests ongoing labor market stabilization
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPI) is stable or declining, indicating sustained monetary accommodation. This low-rate regime compresses equity risk premiums and supports forward equity returns over 60 trading days as investors reach for yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% and CPIAUCSL at 219.146 indicate zero-rate monetary policy with contained inflation
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary accommodation with price stability
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods meeting the trigger condition, or correlation between (FEDFUNDS, CPIAUCSL) joint signal and forward returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline sharply month-over-month, indicating weakening construction demand and reduced near-term economic activity. This signals deteriorating demand momentum and triggers equity selloffs as forward growth expectations contract over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 519k units reflects post-crisis housing recovery plateau
regime_hint: demand-side weakness or construction cycle downturn
failure_condition: Sharp HOUST declines fail to precede negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above trend while unemployment remains elevated, indicating supply-side capacity utilization gains without wage pressure. This creates a favorable earnings environment that drives positive 20-day equity returns as profit margins expand.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.8961 with UNRATE at 9.8% shows recovery with slack
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with labor market slack and rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Joint signal of rising INDPRO and elevated UNRATE fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered periods, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth decelerates to below 0.2% month-over-month, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced discretionary spending. This demand contraction predicts negative 20-day equity returns as forward earnings guidance deteriorates.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 378706M reflects post-crisis consumer spending recovery
failure_condition: Weak RSAFS growth fails to precede negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window while payroll employment stagnates, signaling labor market deterioration and increased recession risk. This dual signal produces negative 60-day equity returns as growth expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp over 3 months AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.8% and PAYEMS at 130539k reflect ongoing post-crisis labor market normalization
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Joint deterioration signal fails to precede negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real short-term interest rates (FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) turn negative and remain below -1.5%, indicating aggressive monetary accommodation. This deeply negative real rate regime drives equity valuations higher over 60 trading days as savers flee cash and bonds.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% with CPIAUCSL at 219.146 implies deeply negative real rates
regime_hint: financial repression or zero-bound monetary policy with positive inflation
failure_condition: Deeply negative real rates fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized in the most recent quarter while retail sales remain flat, indicating demand-side weakness and slowing economic momentum. This growth deceleration predicts negative 60-day equity returns as forward earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13277.4B reflects post-crisis recovery with potential deceleration risk
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or demand-side recession onset
failure_condition: Joint signal of weak GDP growth and flat retail sales fails to precede negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered periods, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
